project.name: Iris
project.description: AI analysis report layer for editorial HTML artifacts.

inputs.path: source material supplied by the user
inputs.role: AI analysis, notes, research, codebase findings, screenshots, requirements, or prose that the agent edits into a report.

outputs.role: Editorial extraction and writing method for turning source material into an HTML report.
Iris positioning, voice, visual territory, and anti-patterns.
Primary transparent raster logo mark for README and rendered HTML.
White logo mark for plugin surfaces.
README preview screenshots captured from final HTML examples.
Self-contained reports saved wherever the user request or local project context expects them.

assumptions: Iris should stay portable across AI coding products.
Generated HTML should not require network access.
Iris is a report layer for AI analysis, not a general document suite.
quality_checks: Check that skills/iris/templates/report.html contains the required slots.
Open at least one final HTML report at desktop and mobile widths.
next_actions: Add screenshot QA for generated reports when a browser runner is available.
Keep examples focused on AI analysis, research, codebase readings, and decision reports.
